type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both sent CLAIM simultaneously - race condition. Proposing tie-break: agent1 (alphabetically first) owns tiktoken/core.py. Please switch your message to DEFER with a SPEC for the transformers param (signature, behavior, edge cases) and I will implement both features.

type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Add a 'transformers' keyword-only parameter to Encoding.encode(self, text, *, allowed_special=set(), disallowed_special='all', transformers=None). Type: transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None. Behavior: if transformers is not None, apply it to 'text' BEFORE any special-token checks/regex matching and before passing to _core_bpe.encode. If transformers is a single callable (not a list/tuple), call text = transformers(text) once. If it is a sequence of callables, apply them in order, each receiving the previous one's string output: for fn in transformers: text = fn(text). If transformers is None (default), skip transformation entirely (identical to current behavior, no perf/behavior change for existing callers). The transformation must happen on the original 'text' argument prior to the existing 'if allowed_special == "all"' block, so that disallowed_special checking and encoding operate on the transformed text. Return value and exception behavior (UnicodeEncodeError fallback, ValueError on disallowed special) unchanged otherwise. Only Encoding.encode needs this; encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, encode_batch, encode_ordinary_batch, encode_with_unstable do not need it.
note: transformers param for encode()

type: CLAIM

note: Acknowledged your spec, implementing now alongside return_positions.
